scope.lane_authority_config_change_description: Adds exactly three filename entries to allowed_path_globs: docs/06_status/T1M_DELEGATION_DESIGN_PACKET.md, docs/06_status/T1M_DELEGATION_CODEX_ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW.md, docs/06_status/T1M_DELEGATION_FINAL_PM_DECISION.md. No wildcard, no directory glob, no neighboring path, and no broader governance-lane authority expansion. This is a configuration change, not a docs-only change; it is narrowly bounded and non-activating (it widens which files a future governance lane may commit, not what CI/Merge Gate/deploy/secrets any lane can do).

pnpm_verify.note: verify:static (sync-check, system-alignment-check, automation-coverage-check, env:check, lint, type-check, build, test, smart-form verify, verify:commands) and pnpm test:live-db both passed.

file_scope_lock.resolution: Griff posted SCOPE_OVERRIDE: APPROVED (schema: scope-override/v1) on PR #1252, bound to exact head f171c67ec0e73c1446983701e79547b833a42a20, naming exactly .lane/lanes/governance.yml -- no wildcard, no broader path. file_scope_lock.history_note: An earlier evidence.json revision at this same PR recorded this check as authoritatively FAIL pending the override -- that was true at the time and is superseded by this resolution, not a retroactive correction of a false claim.

runtime_proof.classification: governance planning documents plus a narrow lane-authority config change
runtime_proof.note: T1 live-DB smoke gate (apps/api/src/database-smoke.test.ts, unchanged by this lane) re-run directly against real Supabase during post-merge truth-close, in addition to the pre-merge pnpm test:live-db run. 7/7 pass, 0 fail, 0 skip. This lane performed no production mutation; the smoke suite creates and cleans up its own test rows. Table/query/count detail below is the deterministic, code-verified behavior of this unchanged smoke suite, re-confirmed by this fresh run.

queries.description: INSERT via processSubmission — one row per synthetic pick, in the 6 of 7 database-smoke tests that create a pick
INSERT via processSubmission (same 6 tests); UPDATE lifecycle_state via transitionPickLifecycle (queued, posted) and via the settlement RPC path

INSERT on each successful transitionPickLifecycle call and on each successful settlement transition; SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero rows were written for a specific to_state after a rejected atomic operation

INSERT via one successful enqueueDistributionAtomic call; UPDATE status to 'processing' via claimNextAtomic; a separate enqueue attempt is rejected and asserted via findByIdempotencyKey returning null
SELECT COUNT(*) used to assert zero rows were written after a rejected confirmDeliveryAtomic call

INSERT via recordPickSettlement/settlePickAtomic on successful calls; one settlePickAtomic call is rejected and asserted via SELECT COUNT(*) returning zero; SELECT used to assert exactly one base record and that a correction does not mutate the original row
SELECT COUNT(*) used by three tests to assert zero audit rows for a rejected atomic operation, and by one test to assert at least two audit rows exist for a successful settlement plus its correction
read-only SELECT (listByType('player')) scanning the full live table for the duplicate (external_id, sport, participant_type) invariant

known_limitations: Merge-type/governance-lane concurrency cap and merge-lock PID-liveness defect (tracked as UTV2-1558, child of UTV2-1553) blocked the standard ops:lane-start / ops:lane-close --repair-merged paths for both this lane's original PR and this post-merge truth-close repair; both were bootstrapped via manually-created worktrees as a documented exception, not resolved by this repair.

No Codex mechanical/technical review of the final diff was completed via CLI (three attempts failed); an automated review bot (chatgpt-codex-connector) independently commented on an earlier head and both findings are addressed in the merged content, with those review threads resolved.

No Grok review occurred at any point (no tool access exists); Fable-equivalent review occurred via an isolated Claude subagent, not a genuine cross-vendor pass. Explicitly disclosed, not held out as equivalent to real cross-vendor independence.

post_merge_truth_close_summary.pm_verdict_comment: PM_VERDICT: APPROVED, schema: pm-verdict/v1, posted by griff843, bound to approved head f171c67e
preserved_truths: The planning-review waiver (Grok/Gemini/Fable) applied only to UTV2-1557 as a non-implementing planning packet. It is not precedent and does not satisfy any future binding T1-M reviewer seat.

The waiver does not apply to implementation (UTV2-1555), bootstrap (UTV2-1451/1546/1500), pilot (UTV2-1556), or activation of machine-authorized T1-M authority -- independent cross-vendor and architecture review remains mandatory before any of those may implement, enable, pilot, or activate.

Ordinary correctness repair limit is two automatic repair rounds (corrected 2026-07-18 in T1M_DELEGATION_FINAL_PM_DECISION.md sec.11 and in live Linear UTV2-1555/UTV2-1556, which previously said three).

Classification disputes, authority vetoes, injection findings, identity anomalies, ledger anomalies, and authority ambiguity escalate immediately with zero automatic repair rounds -- these never consume or reset the two-bounce budget because they never enter the ordinary repair loop.
